description: Fix auth performance - pass server session to SessionProvider to prevent client re-fetch, add time-based DB validation throttling in session callback, and confirm public pages only need lightweight session check
steps: In components/providers.tsx: Add a `session` prop to the Providers component (type it as the next-auth Session type or null). Pass it to <SessionProvider session={session}>. This prevents the client-side SessionProvider from re-fetching /api/auth/session on every mount.

In app/layout.tsx: Pass the already-fetched session to <Providers session={session}>. The session variable already exists from the `auth()` call. Serialize it appropriately for the client (the session object from auth() should be JSON-serializable).

In auth.ts session callback: Add time-based throttling. Store a `lastValidated` timestamp (Unix seconds) in the JWT token. In the session callback, check `Date.now()/1000 - token.lastValidated`. If less than 300 seconds (5 minutes), skip the Prisma lookup and use the cached values from the token directly. If >= 300 seconds OR if lastValidated is missing, do the full Prisma lookup and update the token with fresh data plus a new lastValidated timestamp.

In auth.ts jwt callback: Ensure the jwt callback stores all session-relevant fields (role, name, email, phone, passwordVersion, authVersion, permissions, isActive) in the token on initial sign-in (when `user` is present). Also add `lastValidated: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)` to the token at that point.

In auth.ts session callback: When the DB lookup IS performed (on refresh), update the token fields (role, permissions, etc.) with fresh DB values AND set token.lastValidated = current timestamp. When skipped (within threshold), populate session from token cached values directly.

Ensure the session callback still invalidates the session (sets role to INVALID) if the DB check reveals isActive=false, passwordVersion mismatch, or authVersion mismatch - but only when the check actually runs (every 5 minutes).
acceptance_criteria: SessionProvider in providers.tsx accepts and passes through a session prop
app/layout.tsx passes the server session to Providers
The session callback in auth.ts only queries the database when token.lastValidated is older than 5 minutes
JWT token stores all needed session fields (role, name, email, phone, passwordVersion, authVersion, permissions, isActive, lastValidated)
Security validation (isActive, passwordVersion, authVersion checks) still occurs on every 5-minute refresh
npx next lint passes without errors
